mission.title: podman-container-tools: RFE: Add "rename" subcommand for better Docker compatibility
mission.description: RFE: Add "rename" subcommand for better Docker compatibility. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current podman-container-tools setup
steps.description:
Verify your podman-container-tools version and configuration:
```bash
podman --version
```
This feature requires a working podman-container-tools installation.

steps.title: Review podman-container-tools configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:
# **kind feature**

**Description**

Docker has a "rename" subcommand allowing to change a container name. This feature is missing in podman, and is useful in some cases.

Thank you!

steps.title: Apply the fix for RFE: Add "rename" subcommand for better Docker compatibility
steps.description:
Aliases would work like container names for everything except PS, where
we'd probably need a separate column for them (would be good enough for
grep, but probably break things if you're parsing the output of PS). Of
course, if you wanted to filter to a single container based on name/alias,
we could make that work exactly like it does now

On Mon, Dec 3, 2018, 11:54 Cédric Jeanneret <notifications.com wrote:

>  <https://github.com/baude> I think the id can change - at least, I
> see no reason

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "RFE: Add "rename" subcommand for better Docker compatibility" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Aliases would work like container names for everything except PS, where
we'd probably need a separate column for them (would be good enough for
grep, but probably break things if you're parsing the output of PS).

prerequisites.description: A working podman-container-tools installation or development environment.
